posts.author: Bernd01 (Bernd Hoffmann)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Currently when creating a recipe that checks a dropdown column you can only choose:

  • Contains one of
  • Contains only
  • Contains all

When creating a second step logic check using the automation builder.
There is no “Does NOT contain” in that list, which doesn’t let you check for a negative.
This is a huge limitation for what would be a very powerful feature.
Currently workflows allow for “Does not contain” yet the builder does not.

What are you trying to achieve

I want the ability to choose the “negative” when referencing a dropdown column so that I can stop, or trigger automations when a dropdown column Does Not Contain a label, allowing for hundreds of useful use cases and possible automation builds using forms, creation logic, subitems, and more.

posts.author: KMIntero (Kayla)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would be so helpful! Right now we have several automations that so bulky and it would be much more streamlined to have ‘does not contain’ instead.

Additionally, the option for ‘is/is not empty’ would be great!
